# Transcrição: 01 Lesson #4 Accessing Client Accounts

- **Fonte:** 01 Lesson #4 Accessing Client Accounts
- **Duração:** 03:06
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:00]

All right, so here's how to essentially request access to a client account, ad account. Now if you're just doing an audit or you're just trying to see what they've done, all you need is access to the ad account as an analyst. If you need to advertise, a.k.a. they are an actual client and you're about to set up ads and campaigns, you're going to have to send a request to both the ad account as well as the Facebook page. So you need to send two requests and you should tell the client that they need to approve two notifications, two requests. A lot of clients will just approve one and then there's a lot of back and forth trying to explain to them, oh no, you need to also do the Facebook page. So we're in business manager right now and under your business manager settings, which you can just access through here. And what we'll do is go under pages and what you're going to do is go click add new pages. You're going to request access to a page and you're going to punch in the name of that client, Facebook page. If the name doesn't show up, go to Facebook, bring up the actual page and you'll find that sometimes the URL, so the actual URL of the page is different to the name itself and that's why it's not showing up here. So send that request. So let's say we're doing cat howl or something like that. You'll do page advertiser or well, you don't really need page moderator unless you're doing community management or page editor. You certainly don't need page admin. So usually it's page advertiser. And next, once you send that through, you're going to go to add account and you'll repeat the same process. You're going to go request access to an ad account. Now be very careful that you do not click add an ad account. This is essentially claiming an ad account. So all we want to do is click request ad account. If you claim a page, it will move it into your business manager and that is permanent. So we're going to request ad account and the client is going to give you their ad account ID, which they can find once they're in their ad account. It's a long string of numbers and you're going to enter that number in. So let's just copy this and go like this. And again, access as an advertiser if they're an actual client or simply access as an analyst if you're just doing an audit on this account. Now there's a video that I've provided to you. It's a Dropbox video. You can send that to your client in an email to just explain to them exactly. It's a one minute video how to see those notifications or approve the request if there's no notifications that come through because sometimes notifications don't come through. So you need to request access to the page as well as the ad account.

## [03:00 - 03:06]

If you are advertising, you only need access to the ad account if you're just checking out what they've done with their ads.
